name: Senate Bean Soup
recipeIngredient: 1 lb dried white beans (Great Northern or navy)
1 meaty ham bone or 2 smoked ham hocks
1 cup cooked mashed potatoes or 1/3 cup instant potato flakes
Salt to taste (I found this unnecessary)
1/4 teaspoon freshly ground black pepper, or more to taste
Snippets of parsley or chives for garnish (optional)
recipeInstructions:
1. Rinse and sort the beans, then soak overnight in water to cover.
2. Drain the beans and place them in a 5-quart soup kettle with the ham bones.
3. Add 3 quarts cold water. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer over low heat for 2 hours.
4. Skim if necessary.
5. In the meantime, chop onions, garlic, celery, and parsley.
6. When beans and meat have cooked for 2 hours, add chopped vegetables and potato to the pot.
7. Simmer for 1 hour longer, or until beans are tender. Season with salt and pepper.
8. Remove bones and meat from soup. Chop the meat into half-inch pieces and return to the pot.
9. Discard bones. Reheat soup before serving.
10. Garnish with parsley or chives.
